sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The orange X cross moved right by 3 pixels. The white dot was at (18, 27) and should now be at (18, 30). Let me check where it is now and understand the movement pattern. Since this is a diagonal cross, I need to figure out if UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT move it in cardinal directions or diagonal directions.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']

# White dot position
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'W':
        print(f"White dot: {n['boundary']}")

# Check orange dot count (did any get collected?)
orange_dots = [n for n in seg if n['pixels'] == 1 and n['color'] == 'O']
print(f"Orange dots: {len(orange_dots)}")

# Check if any dots disappeared from gray squares
print("\nGray squares now:")
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'c' and n['pixels'] == 8:
        child_id = n['children'][0] if n['children'] else None
        child = next((c for c in seg if c['id'] == child_id), None)
        child_color = child['color'] if child else 'none'
        child_pos = child['boundary'][0] if child else 'none'
        print(f"  square at {n['boundary']} -> child color={child_color} pos={child_pos}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
White dot moved from (18, 27) to (18, 30) - that's +3 columns (RIGHT). Orange dots went from 47 to 46, so one orange dot was collected. But none of the gray square dots were collected - they're all still there.
So the orange X cross collects orange dots that are part of its own arms as it moves (the trailing dots disappear). This is different from Level 1!
Wait, actually - 47 orange dots were the X cross itself. When it moved right, one orange dot was left behind and disappeared. The cross maintains its shape by consuming its own dots? That seems weird.
Actually, let me reconsider. The orange X cross is made of individual dots. When it moves, the dots shift position. The "collection" is just the cross moving - one dot disappears because the cross shape shifted.
Let me think about this differently. The cross moves 3 pixels per action. The dots in gray squares are the targets. I need to figure out what the cross collects.
Let me try moving the cross so that one of its arms passes through an orange dot in a gray square, like (39, 9) or (42, 24) or (57, 9).
The orange X cross has diagonal arms. From center (18, 30), the arms go:
- Top-left to bottom-right diagonal: (row-col = constant)
- Top-right to bottom-left diagonal: (row+col = constant)
Let me figure out the arm length and which dots would be on the arms.
Actually, let me first undo by moving LEFT back, then try a different approach. Or better, let me just try SPACE to switch to the blue cross and see what happens.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# White dot at (18, 30) now. Orange dots: 46 (one less from the X cross moving)
# The cross moves 3 pixels per step in cardinal directions
# Orange X has diagonal arms, so it collects dots on diagonal lines through its center
#
# Let me try SPACE to switch to blue cross
action(['SPACE'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
